**Digital Camera World** hosts the curated list "The 17 best photography websites."[1] Its buying guide for mobile photography devices is titled **"Best camera phones 2026: The top smartphones for photography, tried and tested by a photographer".**[1]

## References
[1] https://www.inkl.com/news/the-17-best-photography-websites

rounds.answer_b:
**Creative Bloq** authored the guide "The 17 best photography websites," a curated list highlighting top photography resources including Digital Camera World as the top pick.[1] Within Creative Bloq's reviews and features section, their Samsung flagship smartphone review concludes the **Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra** is "excellent for content creators, but offers more of the same," praising its versatile cameras and AI tools while noting incremental upgrades over prior models.[1][4]

**TikTok** has a dedicated "news and features" hub on the Creative Bloq site, focusing on short-form video trends, tips, and platform-specific photography content like TikTok-inspired editing techniques and viral challenges.[1]

The "best camera" buying guide on Creative Bloq targets **intermediate to advanced users**, emphasizing in-depth comparisons of mirrorless models, lens ecosystems, and pro-level features such as dynamic range and autofocus performance, rather than beginner basics.[1]

This digital design and art publication specializes in creative tools, hosting detailed smartphone reviews under its tech and photography sections. The Galaxy S25 Ultra review, published amid 2025-2026 flagships, highlights its 200MP main sensor, 100x zoom, and video prowess for creators, but critiques the familiar design and battery life. TikTok's hub integrates with Creative Bloq's video production coverage, offering tutorials on app-based effects. The camera guide reviews models like Sony Alpha and Canon EOS R series, assuming familiarity with exposure and composition for buyers seeking upgrades.[1][4]

(Word count: 278)

## References
[1] https://www.creativebloq.com/photography/websites-10121096  
[4] https://www.digitalcameraworld.com
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A experienced a catastrophic failure, outputting only citation indices with no text. Agent B correctly identified the publication (Creative Bloq), the specific Samsung model (Galaxy S25 Ultra), and the social hub (TikTok). However, Agent B hallucinated the target audience for the camera guide, claiming it was for 'intermediate to advanced users' while the ground truth specified 'Beginners'. Because the winner contains a factual error on a sub-point, the score is capped at BETTER.
